text.title: compaction-preserve-refs Core Rules
text.summary: Three testable rules for compaction that keep the agent able to resume work without re-reasoning from scratch.

rule.statement: Compacted output MUST preserve verbatim: absolute or workspace-relative file paths, fully-qualified function/class names, error message strings, URLs, IDs (PR numbers, ticket IDs, execution IDs), and explicit decisions ("chose X over Y because Z").
rule.rationale: These are pointers the agent and its downstream tools dereference. Paraphrasing breaks the dereference, and a wrong path is worse than no path because the agent will act on it.

rule.statement: Compacted output MUST drop: intermediate reasoning chains, tool-call argument repetition, verbatim tool output bodies, and conversational filler ("let me think", "ok", "as we discussed").
rule.rationale: Reasoning is regenerable from inputs; tool output is restorable from disk via path. Keeping them inflates the compacted blob and squeezes the surviving references out of the budget.

rule.statement: The compacted state MUST be valid YAML with these top-level keys: goal, files_touched (list), decisions (list), errors_open (list), refs (list of {kind, id}), next_action (one sentence). NO free-text "summary" field is permitted.
rule.rationale: Fixed keys make the output machine-checkable. A missing key is a hard failure the orchestrator catches before the agent acts on bad state; a free-text summary cannot be checked the same way.

rule.statement: When a list has no entries, the compacted state MUST emit an empty list (e.g. `files_touched: []`), never the word "none" or a sentence explaining the absence.
rule.rationale: An empty list is type-correct; "none" is a string that downstream parsers must specially handle, leading to drift between producers and consumers.

rule.statement: The compaction prompt MUST forbid additional top-level keys; the LLM may not add `notes`, `thoughts`, `todo`, or other free-text containers.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc keys drift between calls — one run produces `errors_open`, the next produces `open_errors` — and downstream tooling silently breaks.
